title: Matrix Definitions and Types
summary: Learn what matrices are, how to describe their size, and the special types: square, symmetric, diagonal, identity.
content_html:
A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns, enclosed in brackets. We represent matrices with capital letters.

Size Notation

An m×n matrix has m rows and n columns. Element aij is in row i, column j.

A = [1  2  -5]
    [3  2   0]
    [-1 5   0]   ← 3×3 matrix

Special Matrix Types

  • Square matrix: m = n (same number of rows and columns)
  • Column vector: m×1 matrix
  • Row vector: 1×n matrix
  • Zero matrix (0): all elements are 0
  • Identity matrix (I): square matrix with 1s on the main diagonal and 0s elsewhere
  • Diagonal matrix: square matrix with non-zero elements only on main diagonal
  • Symmetric matrix: A = A^T (transpose equals original)
  • Upper triangular: all elements below main diagonal are 0
  • Lower triangular: all elements above main diagonal are 0

Transpose

The transpose A^T is formed by swapping rows and columns: element (i,j) of A^T = element (j,i) of A.
